narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings, especially after all the effort you've poured into your undergrad years and LSAT prep—it's tough to hear that your law school application didn't make the cut because our model sees a 46% chance you might not pass the bar exam down the line. Picture this as a story the model pieced together from your profile, starting with your Year 3 grades landing you in the 4th decile at school, which stood out as the biggest red flag, whispering that keeping up in the high-stakes world of law might be an uphill battle. That early signal got reinforced by your Year 1 decile of 4.0 too, painting a picture of consistent mid-pack performance that the model worried wouldn't scale to bar-level rigor. Your LSAT score of 25.0 added to that cautious tale, suggesting the analytical edge needed for those grueling exams might need more sharpening. Even your middle-family income bracket played into it, as the model linked it to patterns where extra resources for tutoring or prep sometimes make the difference in tougher outcomes. Your Asian background factored in similarly, tying into historical trends the model has seen toward higher hurdles. Your undergrad GPA of 3.3 nudged things in the same direction, rounding out a profile that felt solidly average rather than standout. On a brighter note, being female and planning full-time study pulled strongly the other way, like quiet strengths the model recognized as boosts toward success in past stories like yours. It's this blend—those top influences stacking toward struggle, just barely outweighed by the positives—that led to the tough call. You're clearly driven, and this isn't the end of your path; maybe targeting schools with stronger bar prep support or boosting that LSAT could rewrite your story next time. Hang in there—you've got the heart for this.
